text.title: Employer Brand Audit Framework
text.summary: Perception-vs-reality audit process using Glassdoor, LinkedIn, candidate surveys, and exit interviews.

rule.statement: Run brand audits annually at minimum. A gap greater than 1.0 on a 5-point scale between internal perception (employee surveys) and external perception (Glassdoor/Comparably) signals repositioning is needed.
rule.rationale: A large perception gap means either the EVP over-promises (candidates arrive disappointed) or the brand under-communicates genuine strengths (missed talent).

list.item: Glassdoor: overall rating, review themes (pros/cons frequency), response rate trend.
LinkedIn: follower growth rate, organic post engagement, career page conversion rate.
Candidate surveys: post-interview NPS, "why declined" categories, source attribution.
Employee surveys: eNPS, top-ranked EVP pillars vs. bottom-ranked, open-text themes.
Exit interviews: top 3 stated reasons for leaving, manager-specific patterns.

rule.statement: Compare each EVP pillar against 3-5 direct talent competitors. Identify where you lead, where you are table-stakes, and where you trail — and never claim leadership where you trail.
rule.rationale: Candidates research employers on Glassdoor and LinkedIn before applying. Positioning claims that contradict visible competitor data undermine recruiter credibility in screening calls.

rule.statement: Respond to every Glassdoor review within 48 hours, including positive ones. Cap responses at 80 words. Never use "We are committed to..." Never dispute employee experience; only describe actions taken or planned.
rule.rationale: Over-defensive responses go viral and amplify negative reviews. Responses that dispute employee perception demonstrate the exact culture problems reviewers described.

antipattern.description: Auto-publishing Glassdoor responses without human approval.
antipattern.reason: Agent-generated responses that contradict employee experience or sound legalistic create PR incidents that are harder to recover from than the original review.

text.reference: Response structure for Glassdoor reviews: acknowledge, describe action, 80-word cap.
